decisions.briefing: Markets posted a quiet, directionless session with both major indices up just 0.3% — no strong catalyst to act on. Our cash sits at $477.14, just below the $500 threshold that would trigger new position-opening, so we're holding the current six-position portfolio and letting existing trades work. With TFPM scoring 100 and several 95-score candidates on deck, we're positioned to move quickly if a sell or cash infusion opens a slot.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities drifted marginally higher on Thursday, with the S&P 500 and NASDAQ each gaining 0.3% in a low-conviction session with no dominant macro catalyst. The mild gain likely reflects month-end positioning and steady institutional rebalancing flows rather than any fresh fundamental development. Investors remain in a holding pattern ahead of upcoming economic data releases, keeping volatility subdued across most sectors.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市週四窄幅上揚，標普500與納斯達克指數均微升0.3%，整體缺乏明確方向性動力。升幅主要來自月末機構再平衡的資金流入，而非基本面出現新變化。投資者普遍持觀望態度，靜候即將公布的宏觀經濟數據，市場波動率維持低位，各板塊表現平穩整固。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag is the performance penalty a portfolio suffers when capital sits uninvested instead of compounding with the market. Holding some cash reduces risk and preserves flexibility, but too much idle cash causes the portfolio to lag a fully invested benchmark like VOO over time. Today's situation is a live example: $477 in cash and four open slots means potential gains are sitting on the sideline rather than working.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」（Cash Drag）是指投資組合中部分資金閒置未進場，導致整體回報落後於市場的現象。適度持有現金有助於降低風險並保留操作靈活性，但過多閒置資金會使組合長期跑輸標普500等全額投入的基準指數。今日情況正是活生生的例子：477美元閒置現金加上四個空倉，意味著潛在收益正在場外白白流失。
